description: Three photos — mountain, forest, water — each posterised into its own number of luminance bands, every band simplified to polygonal masks via Moore boundary tracing and Douglas–Peucker, then clipped from the original image. The resulting slices drift horizontally across the viewport at staggered per-slice speeds, wrapping at the right edge. Dark sky is removed by border flood-fill, blown-out highlights are softly rolled off, and texturally flat regions are filtered out by a luminance-variance threshold.
license_notes: Source photos from Unsplash (Unsplash License, free for commercial use): photo-1545917992-dea2d782ef46 (mountain), photo-1440581572325-0bea30075d9d (forest), photo-1555412654-72a95a495858 (water). No runtime dependencies — pure vanilla HTML/JS with the Canvas 2D and ImageBitmap APIs.
